title: Putting Africa’s Savings to Work
content: Africa’s pension funds and financial institutions hold hundreds of billions of dollars in long-term savings, yet much of that capital flows into sovereign bonds because few scalable alternatives exist. The task now is to build the architecture that channels those savings into productive investments.
snippet: Africa’s pension funds and financial institutions hold hundreds of billions of dollars in long-term savings, yet much of that capital flows into sovereign bonds because few scalable alternatives exist
creator: Gomez Agou
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Great Hungarian Reset
content: Now that he has ousted Viktor Orbán, incoming Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar inherits an economy burdened by a legacy of centralized mismanagement and systemic corruption. To address it, he will need a focused and pragmatic economic program.
snippet: Now that he has ousted Viktor Orbán, incoming Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar inherits an economy burdened by a legacy of centralized mismanagement and systemic corruption. To address it, he wil
creator: Piroska Nagy Mohácsi
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Hormuz Crisis and the Fate of the Global South
content: If the shock from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has revealed economic vulnerabilities, it has also illuminated stark differences in how countries absorb energy-price turbulence. Those that have invested in more resilient clean energy sources are faring better and offering lessons for everyone else.

creator: Laura Carvalho
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Development Doesn’t Prevent War
content: Violent conflicts have reached levels not seen since World War II, even as global poverty has fallen to historic lows, challenging long-held assumptions about the relationship between development and peace. This outcome calls for a reassessment of the theory of change that underpins development aid.
snippet: Violent conflicts have reached levels not seen since World War II, even as global poverty has fallen to historic lows, challenging long-held assumptions about the relationship between development and
creator: Rabah Arezki
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Russians Go Home
content: At a time when illiberalism has presented itself as the wave of the future, Hungarians just voted overwhelmingly to reverse course. But unlike the country’s liberation in 1989, this week’s electoral result represents only the beginning of a longer process, the outcome of which will remain uncertain.
snippet: At a time when illiberalism has presented itself as the wave of the future, Hungarians just voted overwhelmingly to reverse course. But unlike the country’s liberation in 1989, this week’s electoral r
creator: Stephen Holmes
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Trump’s Crypto Push Is Undermining American Power
content: US President Donald Trump’s deregulation of cryptocurrency markets has introduced new financial risks and threatens to undermine the dollar’s global dominance. Russia, Iran, and North Korea have been the biggest beneficiaries, using crypto to evade American sanctions with impunity.
snippet: US President Donald Trump’s deregulation of cryptocurrency markets has introduced new financial risks and threatens to undermine the dollar’s global dominance. Russia, Iran, and North Korea have been
creator: Jayati Ghosh
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Will the IMF Ever Learn?
content: From austerity to taxation, the International Monetary Fund has consistently failed to incorporate its own findings into its lending programs. The Fund's once-a-decade Review of Program Design and Conditionality, which is now underway, offers a critical opportunity to change this.
snippet: From austerity to taxation, the International Monetary Fund has consistently failed to incorporate its own findings into its lending programs. The Fund's once-a-decade Review of Program Design and Con
creator: Timothy Kaldas
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: To Work for Us, AI Must Not Think for Us
content: The public discussion about AI’s impact on society focuses largely on the potential displacement of workers and the loss of jobs. But an even greater risk is the displacement of human thought and the processes that produce the knowledge base on which AI models themselves rely.
snippet: The public discussion about AI’s impact on society focuses largely on the potential displacement of workers and the loss of jobs. But an even greater risk is the displacement of human thought and the
creator: Dani Rodrik
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The World Is Learning to Work Around America
content: The Iran ceasefire has exposed the limits of American power, as the United States was forced to rely on others to contain a crisis of its own making. It offers a glimpse of a possible future in which the countries of the Global South have both the will and the means to navigate crises on their own terms.
snippet: The Iran ceasefire has exposed the limits of American power, as the United States was forced to rely on others to contain a crisis of its own making. It offers a glimpse of a possible future in which
creator: Pedro Abramovay
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What Hungary Can Learn From Poland
content: Following Tisza’s victory over Viktor Orbán’s ruling Fidesz party in Sunday’s election, the opposition faces an uphill battle in trying to restore Hungary’s democracy. Tisza leader Péter Magyar has much to learn from Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk, especially the importance of experimenting with bold solutions.
snippet: Following Tisza’s victory over Viktor Orbán’s ruling Fidesz party in Sunday’s election, the opposition faces an uphill battle in trying to restore Hungary’s democracy. Tisza leader Péter Magyar has mu
creator: Sławomir Sierakowski
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: After Orbán, Hungary Faces an Even Harder Battle
content: Center-right Tisza’s victory in Hungary’s election shows that even a highly entrenched new-right regime can be defeated at the polls. But the myths, resentments, and paranoia that fueled popular support for Viktor Orbán’s brand of illiberalism have not suddenly vanished with his defeat.
snippet: Center-right Tisza’s victory in Hungary’s election shows that even a highly entrenched new-right regime can be defeated at the polls. But the myths, resentments, and paranoia that fueled popular suppo
creator: Maciej Kisilowski
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Hedging Security in the Gulf Is Risky
content: The Gulf states have long hedged their diplomatic and security bets, attempting to strike a balance between those that might protect them from threats (especially the US) and those doing the threatening (such as Iran). But this approach has left them highly vulnerable and must be replaced by a unified approach to allies and foes.
snippet: The Gulf states have long hedged their diplomatic and security bets, attempting to strike a balance between those that might protect them from threats (especially the US) and those doing the threateni
creator: Zaki Laïdi
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Iran War Has Ended a Year of Economic Promise
content: Before US President Donald Trump launched his war of choice against Iran, financial markets were booming in many countries, and private-sector confidence was recovering. But the outlook has suddenly become bleaker, and many governments have only limited policy buffers available to cushion the inflationary shock.
snippet: Before US President Donald Trump launched his war of choice against Iran, financial markets were booming in many countries, and private-sector confidence was recovering. But the outlook has suddenly b
creator: Eswar Prasad
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Africa Is Losing the Iran War
content: The fallout from the latest war in the Middle East has made visible a problem that many preferred to ignore: the international financial architecture is not fit for a world of cascading shocks, tightening fiscal constraints, and rising human need. Nowhere is this more obvious than in Africa.
snippet: The fallout from the latest war in the Middle East has made visible a problem that many preferred to ignore: the international financial architecture is not fit for a world of cascading shocks, tighte
creator: Yemi Osinbajo
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A New Type of Democratic Transition
content: When trying to rebuild after illiberal rule, today’s pro-democracy governments discover booby traps laid by their predecessors and confront all kinds of veto players. But Poland’s experience has been instructive, highlighting the need for bold action and the right support from external actors.
snippet: When trying to rebuild after illiberal rule, today’s pro-democracy governments discover booby traps laid by their predecessors and confront all kinds of veto players. But Poland’s experience has been
creator: Jan-Werner Mueller
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How to Understand Emerging Risks
content: Understanding and anticipating risks is crucial to protecting society from the unpredictable. But the nature of risk is evolving at an unprecedented pace, and the processes and assumptions that guide insurers and their clients in managing uncertainty must be adjusted accordingly.
snippet: Understanding and anticipating risks is crucial to protecting society from the unpredictable. But the nature of risk is evolving at an unprecedented pace, and the processes and assumptions that guide
creator: Françoise Gilles
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: America’s War Is Adding to Africa’s Debt Burden
content: The US-Israeli war against Iran has shown, once again, that when American policy decisions push Treasury yields higher, African sovereigns pay the price. African governments must ensure that this is the last geopolitical crisis that drives up their borrowing costs through no fault of their own.
snippet: The US-Israeli war against Iran has shown, once again, that when American policy decisions push Treasury yields higher, African sovereigns pay the price. African governments must ensure that this is t
creator: Vera Songwe
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Decline and Fall of the Dollar Empire
content: The United States does not have an emperor like ancient Rome's Nero, but its politics are increasingly subject to rule by one man who threatens its democratic traditions. And just as Nero undermined the Roman denarius—arguably the world's first international currency—America's ruler poses a growing threat to his country's currency.
snippet: The United States does not have an emperor like ancient Rome's Nero, but its politics are increasingly subject to rule by one man who threatens its democratic traditions. And just as Nero undermined t
creator: Barry Eichengreen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Next UN Secretary-General Must Be a Woman
content: As United Nations member states prepare to elect a new secretary-general, they should break with precedent and choose a woman. This would not be a corrective gesture or a form of symbolic representation, but rather a strategic acknowledgment that leadership selection must reflect the realities of contemporary governance.
snippet: As United Nations member states prepare to elect a new secretary-general, they should break with precedent and choose a woman. This would not be a corrective gesture or a form of symbolic representati
creator: Vaira Vīķe-Freiberga
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Hungary’s New Leader Must Avoid Joe Biden’s Mistakes
content: If Hungary can become a model for illiberal movements elsewhere to emulate, it can also become a sandbox for experimenting with democratic renewal. America and others suffering democratic backsliding can offer lessons for Péter Magyar's new government, whose successes or failures, in turn, will generate new lessons.
snippet: If Hungary can become a model for illiberal movements elsewhere to emulate, it can also become a sandbox for experimenting with democratic renewal. America and others suffering democratic backsliding
creator: Aziz Huq
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Why Orbán Lost
content: The downfall of Hungary's autocratic prime minister, whose model of "illiberal democracy"  became a lodestar to many on the right—was a consequence of that model's own logic. Whatever happens next, one thing is certain: democracy's friends and foes alike will be watching closely.
snippet: The downfall of Hungary's autocratic prime minister, whose model of "illiberal democracy" became a lodestar to many on the right—was a consequence of that model's own logic. Whatever happens next, one
creator: László Bruszt
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Iran War’s Winners and Losers
content: The current ceasefire allows us to make a preliminary assessment of the war and its effects. China and Russia were clear winners, while the Gulf Arab states suffered a reputational hit, and the combatants all had decidedly mixed outcomes, although the United States and Israel gained the least.

creator: Richard Haass
